events.label: assistant
events.text: I’m going to write the hazard assessment directly to `/app/answer.json`, using the gas-code bonding requirements and the in-progress service context.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la /app && test -f /app/answer.json && sed -n '1,160p' /app/answer.json || true"
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:47 ..

events.label: assistant
events.text: The existing workspace has no prior answer file, so I’m creating a new JSON document with the required schema and only the fields that apply populated.

events.label: assistant
events.text: The file is in place. I’m doing a quick JSON parse check now so the output is valid and can be consumed as-is.

events.label: assistant
events.text: `jq` is not installed in this container, so I’m using the standard JSON tooling available in the runtime for the same parse-only validation.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/tmp/answer.pretty && sed -n '1,220p' /tmp/answer.pretty"
